mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading below 7.0 percent confirms robust labor demand, signaling higher consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that drives positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and housing sector expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month during a post-recession recovery phase indicates expanding capacity utilization without immediate inflationary constraints, supporting positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.0% MoM coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below baseline during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent signals accelerating inflation, which forces central banks to tighten policy and compresses equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3%, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth consistently outpaces real GDP growth over consecutive quarters, labor cost inflation erodes corporate operating margins, generating negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth rates above 2.5 percent quarter-over-quarter reflect strong aggregate demand that directly translates into higher corporate earnings revisions, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% QoQ is followed by negative 20-day forward returns in over 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and earnings expectations, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of months following two consecutive declines in INDPRO, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample tests.
